aria-label=3D"simple banner" style=3D"background: li= near-gradient(0deg, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7), rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7)), ur= l(/pb-assets/images/authors/author-center-banner-bg-1633723115053.png) no-r= epeat center; background-size: cover;" class=3D"simple-banner"><h2 class=3D= "main-title text-center mb-5 py-5 text-capitalize border-top border-bottom"= >PNAS Author Center</h2></section> =20 <section class=3D"container-fluid mb-5 bg-white authorcenter"> =20 =20 <div data-widget-def=3D"ux3-layout-widget" data-widget-id=3D"0066ba= 36-be09-48f0-acbd-7272c2773a7f" class=3D"row"> =20 =20 <div class=3D"col-lg-3 check-btn"> =20 <input id=3D"myid" type=3D"checkbox"> <label class=3D"menu-toggle-b= utton" for=3D"myid">Menu</label> =20 =20 <div data-widget-def=3D"UX3AccordionWidget" data-widget-id=3D"dc5c8= 3d7-853a-422f-84ce-8fe5801c0fbe" class=3D"navlist-ac menu-toggle pb-3 pb-md= -0"> =20 =20 <div class=3D"accordion"> <ul class=3D"author-center__side-menu lis= t-inline"><li><a class=3D"author-center__side-menu__title" href=3D"https://= www.pnas.org/author-center">Author Center Home</a><div id=3D"ad424d6f6-897d= -4efb-bbac-142818e52141" class=3D"collapse"></div></li><li><a class=3D"auth= or-center__side-menu__title" href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/cal= l-for-papers">Call for Papers</a><div id=3D"a58132de6-cf2f-4e31-a696-f4f2af= 0cdd9a" class=3D"collapse"></div></li><li><a class=3D"author-center__side-m= enu__title" href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/submitting-your-manu= script#2726807c-2ad6-4c01-867c-990ae463d52a" data-toggle=3D"collapse" data-= target=3D"#a2726807c-2ad6-4c01-867c-990ae463d52a">Submitting Your Manuscrip= t</a><div id=3D"a2726807c-2ad6-4c01-867c-990ae463d52a" class=3D"collapse"> =20 <ul class=3D"menu-ac"> <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/submitting-your-manuscri= pt#article-types">Article Types</a></li> <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/submitting-your-manuscri= pt#initial-submissions">Initial Submissions</a></li> <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/submitting-your-manuscri= pt#revised-submissions">Revised Submissions</a></li> <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/submitting-your-manuscri= pt#manuscript-formatting-guidelines">Manuscript Formatting Guidelines</a></= li> <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/submitting-your-manuscri= pt#journal-cover-images">Journal Cover Images</a></li> </ul> </div></li><li><a class=3D"author-center__side-menu__title" href=3D"https:/= /www.pnas.org/author-center/member-contributed-submissions">Member-Contribu= ted Submissions</a><div id=3D"a32ac9595-5b0c-4ef9-8ea7-eea96e4aa683" class= =3D"collapse"></div></li><li><a class=3D"author-center__side-menu__title" h= ref=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/editorial-and-journal-policies">E= ditorial and Journal Policies</a><div id=3D"a2c49d8c8-34e5-4dff-aa92-af6f51= a8a13e" class=3D"collapse"></div></li><li><a class=3D"author-center__side-m= enu__title" href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/publication-ethics-p= rocess">Publication Ethics Process</a><div id=3D"ab61e3ad6-8094-44fb-a4da-5= 8564bbc372d" class=3D"collapse"></div></li><li><a class=3D"author-center__s= ide-menu__title" href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/publication-cha= rges">Publication Charges</a><div id=3D"ab2ce21ca-a86f-4fe6-9633-9d53cf0663= 70" class=3D"collapse"></div></li><li><a class=3D"author-center__side-menu_= _title" href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/after-acceptance">After = Acceptance</a><div id=3D"aede9fedc-be0f-4392-862c-1f3bc8c8115b" class=3D"co= llapse"></div></li><li><a class=3D"author-center__side-menu__title" href=3D= "https://www.pnas.org/author-center/pnas-nexus">PNAS Nexus</a><div id=3D"ab= ee914b0-a427-4d96-8f21-21c2ac07bea1" class=3D"collapse"></div></li></ul></d= iv> </div> =20 </div><div class=3D"col-lg-9 col-md-12"> =20 <!--begin pagefulltext--> =20 =20 <h1>Submitting Your Manuscript</h1> <p>PNAS welcomes submissions in all scientific disciplines from researchers= worldwide. Information on submitting your manuscript is included below. Du= e to the volume of submissions received, PNAS does not consider presubmissi= on inquiries. Please contact us if you have any questions about the submiss= ion process. <a href=3D"https://academic.oup.com/pnasnexus/pages/general-in= structions">Click here</a> for information about submitting to <i>PNAS Nexu= s</i>.</p> <p><a class=3D"btn btn-primary mt-2 signup-alert-ad__action" href=3D"https:= //www.pnascentral.org/cgi-bin/main.plex">Submit Your Manuscript</a> <a clas= s=3D"btn btn-primary mt-2 signup-alert-ad__action" href=3D"mailto:pnas@nas.= edu?subject=3DQuestion%20regarding%20PNAS%20Submission%20#[Enter%20ID%20Her= e]">Contact Us</a></p> =20 <a id=3D"article-types" name=3D"article-types"></a> <h2>PNAS Article Types</h2> <div class=3D"card-ac"> <h5 class=3D"card-header-ac">Unsolicited article types</h5> <div class=3D"card-body-ac"> <p><strong>Research Reports</strong> describe the results o= f original research of exceptional importance. The preferred length of thes= e articles is 6 pages, but PNAS allows articles up to a maximum of 12 pages= . For example, a standard 6-page article is approximately 4,000 words, 50 r= eferences, and 4 medium-size graphical elements (i.e., figures and tables).= </p> <ul> <li><strong>Direct Submissions</strong>: Most PNAS arti= cles are Direct Submissions.</li> <li><strong>Contributed submissions:</strong> NAS membe= rs may contribute two research manuscripts annually within their area of ex= pertise in which they had a direct, significant role in the design and exec= ution of the work. Learn more about the <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/aut= hor-center/member-contributed-submissions">Contributed submissions</a> proc= ess.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Brief Reports</strong> describe observations of = immediate impact that may hold potential to initiate new avenues of researc= h, provide compelling new data on controversies of broad interest and long-= standing questions, or present a concise conceptual advance.</p> <ul> <li><strong>All Brief Reports are published immediate o= pen access=E2=80=8B.</strong></li> <li>Brief Reports are limited to 3 pages, which is appr= oximately 1,600 words (including the manuscript text, title page, abstract,= and figure legends), and 15 references.</li> <li>Supporting information (SI) is limited to extended = methods, essential supporting datasets, and videos (no additional tables or= figures).</li> <li>All Brief Reports follow the <a href=3D"https://www= .pnas.org/author-center/editorial-and-journal-policies">Direct Submission</= a> mode of review and are not eligible as member-contributed submissions.</= li> </ul> <p><strong>Letters to the Editor</strong> provide brief com= ments that allow readers to constructively address a difference of opinion = with authors of a PNAS article.</p> <ul> <li>Letters must be submitted within six months of the = first online publication date of the subject article.</li> <li>Letters are limited to 500 words, 2 graphical elements (figures or= tables), and 10 references. Legends should only include brief descriptions= of the figures. Supporting information is not allowed. Data and code used = to support the Letter may be posted in a repository and cited in the Letter= . Additional discussion, tables, or figures are not allowed.</li> <li>Letters may not include references to submitted pap= ers or unpublished results, requests to cite the Letter writer's work, accu= sations of misconduct, calls for retraction, or personal comments to an aut= hor.</li> <li>Letters typically publish alongside an Author Respo= nse from the authors of the subject article.</li> <li>PNAS will consider Letters for Brief Report, Contributed, Direct S= ubmission, Inaugural, Opinion, and Perspective articles.</li> </ul> =20 <p><strong>Opinions</strong> are succinct essays that = appear in the Front Matter section. Authored by researchers, Opinion pieces= further the discourse on a timely topic or offer a call to action through = a clearly articulated argument that presents novel ideas or proposals. = ;Opinions are not summaries or synopses; the essay=E2=80=99s argu= ment should be explicitly stated early on, and then supported in the t= ext. Submissions should also address relevant counter-arguments. All a= uthors are welcome to submit proposals for consideration. We especiall= y welcome a broad range of voices and contributors. Submissions deemed= appropriate for the section are reviewed by at least one NAS member, or ot= her expert, who has the relevant expertise. Submissions should be approxima= tely 1,500=E2=80=932,000 words, not including references and do not require= abstracts.</p> </div> </div> <div class=3D"card-ac"> <h5 class=3D"card-header-ac">By invitation only</h5> <div class=3D"card-body-ac"> <p><strong>Commentaries</strong> call attention to articles= of particular note.</p> <p><strong><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/topic/badge/inau= gural">Inaugural Articles</a></strong> provide newly elected NAS members an= opportunity to introduce themselves to the readers of PNAS. Inaugural Arti= cles may be submitted by newly elected NAS members within five years of ele= ction. These are predominantly original research articles that may contain = new ideas or hypotheses or describe the historical development of the membe= r=E2=80=99s field. They may contain a personal perspective component, but R= eview articles are not considered.</p> <p><strong><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/front-matter">Fr= ont Matter</a></strong> is a magazine section that tells the stories of sci= ence. Sections include News Features, Science and Culture, Inner Workings,&= nbsp;Core Concepts, QnAs, Profiles, and the <em>Science Sessions</em> = podcast. All of these elements are written or produced by science writers a= nd journalists who receive assignments from PNAS editors.</p> <p><strong><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/topic/pers">Pers= pectives</a></strong> should identify a critical science problem, provide a= state-of-the-art assessment, and offer new insights or a new approach to i= ts resolution. The narrative style may vary, but each Perspective should fo= cus on an important area of research and be accessible to a broad scientifi= c audience. Perspectives may help contextualize findings within a field or = add a new dimension to previously published research. Current advances and = novel insights are encouraged. To submit a proposed article, select =E2=80= =9CPresubmission for Perspective.=E2=80=9D Invited Perspectives are reviewe= d as described in the <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/editori= al-and-journal-policies#peer-review-process">Peer Review Process</a>. PNAS = allows up to a maximum of 10 pages for these articles.</p> =09 <p><strong>Author Responses</strong> are invited at the request of the = Editorial Board. They briefly respond to points raised in Letters to the Ed= itor regarding PNAS articles published in the past six months.</p> =09 <ul> <li>Author Responses are limited to 500 words, 2 graphical elements (f= igures or tables), and 11 references, including one reference to the associ= ated Letter and one to the subject article. Legends should only include bri= ef descriptions of the figures. Supporting information is not allowed. Data= and code used to support the Response may be posted in a repository and ci= ted in the Response. Additional discussion, tables, or figures are not allo= wed.</li> <li>Author Responses will typically publish alongside the associated L= etter to the Editor.</li> </ul> =09 <p><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/about/special-features">= <strong>Special Features</strong></a> in PNAS are carefully curated collect= ions of articles that explore significant topics in areas of interest to PN= AS readers and make important contributions to the science and literature. = Proposals for Special Features are reviewed by Editorial Board members and = other subject matter experts to ensure they present a cohesive and balanced= collection that will be of interest to a broad audience. Topics of Special= Features are often interdisciplinary and may be in emergent fields or thos= e underrepresented in PNAS. Papers invited as part of a Special Feature und= ergo the same rigorous review process as Direct Submissions and Perspective= s and publish together within a regular weekly issue. PNAS is committed to = broad participation in the organization and authorship of Special Features.= All Special Feature organizers and participating authors must declare any = competing interests.</p> =20 <p><strong>Colloquium Papers</strong> are reports of scient= ific colloquia held under NAS auspices.</p> </div> </div> =20 <a id=3D"initial-submissions" name=3D"initial-submissions"></a> <h2 style=3D"margin-top: 20px;">Initial Submissions</h2> <p>PNAS follows a format-neutral policy for initial submissions, meaning yo= ur manuscript does not need to adhere to strict journal formatting guidelin= es to be considered. However, we do require the following information:</p> <ol> <li>a manuscript file (in any format) including the following: <ul> <li>title page (title, author list, classification, keywords)</= li> <li>abstract</li> <li>significance statement</li> <li>main text</li> <li>references</li> <li>figures or tables with appropriate legends (may be uploaded= separately)</li> <li>SI files (may be uploaded separately)</li> </ul> </li> <li>contact and competing interest information for all authors</li> <li>data sharing plans (for all data, documentation, and code used in a= nalysis)</li> <li>funding information and whether an open access license has been sel= ected</li> <li>list of appropriate <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/about/editorial= -board"> Editorial Board members</a>, <a href=3D"https://nrc88.nas.edu/pnas= _search/default.aspx"> NAS members</a>, and qualified reviewers (minimum of= three each) who are experts in the paper's scientific area. (A b= rief justification for suggested reviewers is welcome, particularly for int= erdisciplinary papers)</li> </ol> <p>PNAS will consider manuscripts for review as long as all the components = listed above are included in your submission. For more detailed guidance on= manuscript formatting, including what to include in each section of your f= ile, please see the <strong>Manuscript Formatting Guidelines</strong> below= . </p> <p><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/member-contributed-submiss= ions">To learn more about the initial submission process for member-contrib= uted submissions, click here.</a> Please note that the final version of the= paper must be submitted by the last day of the year to count toward that y= ear's annual limit.</p> =20 <a id=3D"revised-submissions" name=3D"revised-submissions"></a> <h2>Revised Submissions</h2> <p><strong>Revised papers must be submitted within two months of receiving = the revision decision or they will be treated as new submissions.</strong> = If you need more time, please notify <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/about/= staff-directory">PNAS</a>. Along with the information provided at initial s= ubmission, revised submissions must also include:</p> <ul> <li>a revised version of the manuscript, and</li> <li>a point-by-point response to reviewer comments</li> </ul> <p>The revised version of the manuscript should be uploaded as a Manuscript= file type. Authors are strongly encouraged to include a tracked-changes fi= le for review purposes. Revised submissions are encouraged to adhere to all=  <strong>Manuscript Formatting Guidelines</strong>, including the <str= ong>Publication-Ready Source File Guidelines</strong>. Please note that mul= tiple revisions are rarely permitted, and acceptance of the paper is not gu= aranteed. If you have any questions regarding manuscript formatting or the = revision process, please <a href=3D"mailto:pnas@nas.edu">contact us</a> for= assistance.</p> <a id=3D"manuscript-formatting-guidelines" name=3D"manuscript-formatting-gu= idelines"></a> <h2>Manuscript Formatting Guidelines</h2> <p>Read the <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/submitting-your-m= anuscript#initial-submissions">Initial Submission Guidelines</a> to le= arn about formatting requirements for submitting to PNAS. Before preparing = your revised submission, be sure to review the <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.= org/author-center/submitting-your-manuscript#revised-submissions">Revised S= ubmission Guidelines</a>. Detailed formatting instructions are provided bel= ow.</p> <h3>Manuscript templates</h3> <p>Please use the templates below to prepare your manuscript for PNAS.</p> <table class=3D"table"> <thead> <tr style=3D"background-color: #f5f5f5; border-bottom: 1px solid #5= B5B5B;"> <th>Word</th> <th>LaTeX (Overleaf)</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr>=20 <td><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/PNASTempl= ateforMainManuscript-1755285180130.docx">Research article</a></td> <td><a href=3D"https://www.overleaf.com/latex/templates/templat= e-for-preparing-your-research-report-submission-to-pnas-using-overleaf/fzcb= zjvpvnxn">Research article</a></td> </tr> <tr>=20 <td><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/PNASTempl= ateBR-1755285179960.docx">Brief Report</a></td> <td><a href=3D"https://www.overleaf.com/latex/templates/templat= e-for-preparing-your-research-report-submission-to-pnas-using-overleaf-2025= /whbdryzwztnd">Brief Report</a></td> </tr> <tr>=20 <td><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/PNAStempl= ateSI-1755285180080.docx">SI Appendix</a></td> <td><a href=3D"https://www.overleaf.com/latex/templates/pnas-te= mplate-for-supplementary-information/wqfsfqwyjtsd#.WrAIzcPwZEZ">SI Appendix= </a></td> </tr> <tr style=3D"border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;"></tr> </tbody> </table> <p>Support for LaTeX templates is available through <a href=3D"https://www.= overleaf.com/contact">Overleaf</a>. If you have questions about submitting = your manuscript in LaTeX, please <a href=3D"mailto:pnas@nas.edu">contact PN= AS</a> and include the manuscript file as an attachment when possible.</p> =20 <h3>Manuscript order</h3> <p>Submitting your manuscript sections in the following order will help us = to locate key information more easily. Please number all manuscript pages s= tarting with the title page. The recommended order is:</p> <ol> <li>Title page</li> <li>Abstract <ul> <li>Explain to the general reader the major contributions of the article</l= i> <li>Include no more than 250 words</li> <li>Cite all references in the abstract in full within the abstract itself = AND in the text</li> </ul> </li> <li>Significance statement (Direct and Contributed Submissions only) <ul> <li>Explain the significance of the research at a level understandable to a= n undergraduate-educated scientist outside their field of specialty</li> <li>Include no more than 120 words</li> </ul> </li> <li>Main text <ul> <li>Introduction</li> <li>Results</li> <li>Discussion</li> <li>Materials and methods (describe procedures in sufficient detail so that= the work can be repeated)</li> </ul> </li> <li>Acknowledgments and funding sources <ul> <li>Spell out all abbreviations</li> <li>Use <a href=3D"https://www.crossref.org/services/funder-registry/">Fund= Ref</a> to identify the standard name for any funders</li> <li>Do not include dedications</li> </ul> </li> <li>References</li> <li>Figure legends</li> </ol> =20 <h3>Title page element</h3> <p>Please include the following information on the title page:</p> <table class=3D"table"> <thead> <tr style=3D"background-color: #f5f5f5; border-bottom: 1px solid #5= B5B5B;"> <th>Title Page Element</th> <th>Description</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Title</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>Keep the title brief (< 135 characters), descriptive,= and comprehensible to a broad scientific audience. Include the studied org= anism. Avoid numbers, acronyms, abbreviations, punctuation, and puns. = </p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Author line</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>Include full names of all authors in the order intended = for publication.</p> <p>Use asterisks (*) to designate co-corresponding authors = and numbered footnotes to indicate equal contributions.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Author affiliations</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>Include, in this order, department/laboratory/section/di= vision, institution, city, state with ZIP code (for US institutions), or co= untry with postal code (for non-US institutions).</p> <p>Use superscripts to match authors with institutions. Mul= tiple affiliations are allowed. Affiliations where the work was done should= be listed. If an author's affiliation has changed since the work was done,= the current affiliation should also be listed and noted as such.</p> <p>PNAS requires the corresponding author to supply an ORCI= D identifier at submission and strongly encourages all authors to use an OR= CID ID. Do not include ORCIDs in the manuscript file; individual authors mu= st link their ORCID account to their PNAS profile at <a href=3D"https://www= .pnascentral.org/">www.pnascentral.org</a>. For proper authentication, auth= ors are not permitted to add ORCIDs on proofs. <a href=3D"https://orcid.org= /">Learn more or register for ORCID</a>.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Corresponding author</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>Include the name, complete address, phone number, and em= ail address of the author(s) to whom correspondence and proofs should be se= nt.</p> <p>Note that corresponding authors' email addresses will be= published in the article footnotes.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Preprint servers</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>If applicable, note if a manuscript was deposited as a p= reprint, including to which server(s) and the licensing information in rega= rds to the article.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Classification</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>Select both a major and minor classification catego= ry. Dual classifications are permitted between major categories.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Keywords</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>Include at least three keywords at submission.</p> </td> </tr> <tr style=3D"border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;"></tr> </tbody> </table> =20 <a id=3D"classifications" name=3D"classifications"></a> <h3>Classifications</h3> <p>Select a major (Physical, Social, or Biological Sciences) and a minor ca= tegory from the following. Dual classifications are permitted between major= categories. Dual classifications within a major category are only permitte= d in exceptional cases and are subject to Editorial Board approval.</p= > <div class=3D"card-ac"> <h5 class=3D"card-header-ac">Physical Sciences</h5> <div class=3D"card-body-ac"> <p>Applied Mathematics; Applied Physical Sciences; Astronom= y; Biophysics and Computational Biology; Chemistry; Computer Sciences; Eart= h, Atmospheric, and Planetary Sciences; Engineering; Environmental Sciences= ; Mathematics; Physics; Statistics; and Sustainability Science.</p> </div> </div> <div class=3D"card-ac"> <h5 class=3D"card-header-ac">Social Sciences</h5> <div class=3D"card-body-ac"> <p>Anthropology; Demography; Economic Sciences; Environment= al Sciences; Political Sciences; Psychological and Cognitive Sciences; Soci= al Sciences; and Sustainability Science.</p> </div> </div> <div class=3D"card-ac"> <h5 class=3D"card-header-ac">Biological Sciences</h5> <div class=3D"card-body-ac"> <p>Agricultural Sciences; Anthropology; Applied Biological = Sciences; Biochemistry; Biophysics and Computational Biology; Cell Biology;= Developmental Biology; Ecology; Environmental Sciences; Evolution; Genetic= s; Immunology and Inflammation; Medical Sciences; Microbiology; Neuroscienc= e; Pharmacology; Physiology; Plant Biology; Population Biology; Psychologic= al and Cognitive Sciences; Sustainability Science; and Systems Biology.</p> </div> </div> =20 <h3 style=3D"margin-top: 20px;">Main text</h3> <p>Please take note of the following when preparing your manuscript for PNA= S:</p> <ul> <li>Do not include statements of novelty or priority.</li> <li>Avoid laboratory jargon.</li> <li>Use correct chemical names.</li> <li>Specify strains of organisms.</li> <li>Display trade names with an initial capital letter only.</li> <li>Provide names of suppliers of uncommon reagents or instruments.</li> <li>Use Syst=C3=A8me International units and symbols whenever possible.</li= > <li>Only link to websites that are permanent public repositories, such as s= elf-perpetuating online resources funded by government, academia, and indus= try. Links to an author's personal web page are not acceptable.</li> <li>Use standardized nomenclature for species-specific gene and protein nam= es (see <a href=3D"https://www.genecards.org/">Genecards</a>, <a href=3D"ht= tp://www.informatics.jax.org/mgihome/nomen/index.shtml"> MGI Nomenclature p= age</a>, <a href=3D"https://www.genenames.org/"> HUGO Gene Nomenclature Com= mittee</a>, <a href=3D"https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00122-023= -04253-w">the Wheat Initiative</a>, or equivalent resources). <ul> <li>Proposed gene names must be deposited to and approved by the appropriat= e nomenclature committee before publication of the article.</li> </ul> </li> <li>Ensure that abbreviations are accessible to a broad scientific audience= . Define all abbreviations that are specific to a particular field at first= mention in the text.</li> <li>Use international standards on nomenclature. PNAS uses <em> Scientific = Style and Format: The CSE Manual for Authors, Editors, and Publishers </em>= (8th edition, 2014) as the primary style guide.</li> <li>If you believe your manuscript would benefit from professiona= l editing, we encourage you to use an editing service (see list <a hre= f=3D"https://www.pnas.org/author-center/language-editing">here</a>) prior t= o submission. PNAS does not take responsibility for or endorse these servic= es, and their use has no bearing on acceptance of a manuscript for publicat= ion.</li> <li>We encourage authors to follow the <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-a= ssets/authors/TRRP_Best-Practices-for-Researchers-1667916710883.pdf">Text R= ecycling Research Project=E2=80=99s Best Practices for Researchers</a></li> </ul> <h3>Publication-ready source files guidelines</h3> <p>Once your manuscript is accepted, PNAS will request publication-ready so= urce files. Please follow the guidelines below when preparing these files. = Extensive edits are not permitted at this stage; only minor edits will be a= llowed during the proofing process.</p> <h3>Manuscript file</h3> <p>Manuscript files must meet the following criteria:</p> <p>Accepted formats:</p> <ul> <li>Word, RTF, or LaTeX format</li> <li>No embedded figures, tables, or schemes</li> <li>Equations must be editable; images are not acceptable</li> <li>In-text footnotes should be indicated with symbols in this order: *, = =E2=80=A0, =E2=80=A1, =C2=A7, =C2=B6, #, ||,**, =E2=80=A0=E2=80=A0, =E2=80= =A1=E2=80=A1, =C2=A7=C2=A7, =C2=B6=C2=B6, ##</li> </ul> =20 <a id=3D"latex" name=3D"latex"></a> <h3>LaTeX</h3> <p>The PNAS submission system runs PDFLaTeX (Overleaf=E2=80=99s default). P= lease use the latest version of LaTeX2e to prepare your files. Using other = versions or alternate engines (such as XeLaTeX or LuaLaTeX) may cause compi= lation issues at submission. </p> <ul>=20 <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/PNAS-template-mai= n-1764608845890.tex">PNAS LaTeX template</a></li> <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/pnas-new-17646088= 46380.cls">Main class file</a></li>=20 <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/pnasresearchartic= le-1764608846380.sty">Main style file</a></li>=20 <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/widetext-16334603= 30490.sty">Equations spanning two columns</a></li> <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/pnas-new-17646088= 45930.bst">Bibliography style file</a></li>=20 <li><a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/pnas_additional_l= atex_instructions-1633460324960.pdf">Additional LaTeX Instructions</a></li> </ul> <h4>Basic rules</h4> <ul> <li>Upload .cls, .sty, .bib, .bst, .bbl, and .ldf files using the =E2= =80=9CLaTeX Support File=E2=80=9D file type. The PNAS submissions syst= em treats each LaTeX submission as a single directory.</li> <li>Avoid nesting, cross-referencing, and using input commands, as thes= e will cause compilation errors.</li> <li>Do not remove packages that are included in the Overleaf template.<= /li> <li>When adding packages to the template, ensure that their comman= ds do not clash with existing packages.</li> </ul> <h4>Figures and tables</h4> <ul> <li>Use the \includegraphics command within the main manuscript file an= d upload each figure source file in the submission system separately using = the =E2=80=9CFigure=E2=80=9D file type.</li> <li>Include tables within the main manuscript .tex file only.</li> </ul> <h4>References</h4> <ul> <li>Use <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/pnas-new-1764= 608845930.bst">pnas-new.bst</a> for the current PNAS bibliography style.</l= i> <li>Use the Automatic Journal Title Abbreviation package provided in Ov= erleaf to prevent references from appearing longer than they would in = publication.</li> </ul> =20 <h3>Resolving compilation problems in submission</h3> <ul> <li>To recompile the manuscript file, delete the original file from the sub= mission and upload it again.</li> <li>Upload an alternate PDF as a =E2=80=9CRelated Manuscript File=E2=80=9D = if the system compiled PDF is incomplete or inaccurate.</li> </ul> <h3>Figures</h3> <p>Supply figures and schemes as high-resolution files whenever possible. P= lease review the <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/authors/digitala= rt-1675347574760.pdf"> PNAS Digital Art Guidelines</a> before uploading ima= ges.</p> <p>PNAS strives to ensure articles are accessible for readers throughout th= e world by offering article PDFs that are easily suitable for downloading i= n any environment. In order to accommodate size and content, article PDFs a= re processed to display images at 200 ppi, which will guarantee quality at = print size. The HTML display of an article offers 300 ppi for all images. I= n addition, the option to enlarge each figure and table in the HTML display= is available to closely review comprehensive details, as necessary.</p> <ul> <li>Provide all images at final size. While figures may be sized conservati= vely to save page space, PNAS reserves the right to make the final decision= on figure size in published articles and authors may be asked to shorten m= anuscripts that exceed the stated length requirement. <ul> <li>Small: approximately 9 cm x 6 cm</li> <li>Medium: approximately 11 cm x 11 cm</li> <li>Large: approximately 18 cm x 22 cm</li> </ul> </li> <li>Ensure that all numbers, letters, and symbols are no smaller than = 6 points (2 mm) and no larger than 12 points (6 mm) after reduction. Keep t= ext sizing consistent within each graphic.</li> <li>Preassemble all composite figures. </li> <li>Submit images in these file formats: TIFF, EPS, PDF, or PPT.</li> <li>Submit 3D images as either PRC or U3D. For each 3D image, include a&nbs= p;2D representation in TIFF, EPS, or PDF format.</li> </ul> <h4>Alternative text</h4> <p>Beginning with Volume 123, PNAS includes alternative text, or alt text, = for all journal figures in the version of record hosted on the PNAS website= . Alt text is a short description of an image that conveys essential contex= t to readers using screen readers and improves digital accessibility. Alt t= ext is generated during production using generative AI technology and is re= viewed by a scientific editor to ensure accuracy. To learn more about acces= sibility at PNAS, including a link to download the latest Voluntary Product= Accessibility Template, please visit our <a href=3D"https://www.pnas.org/a= bout/accessibility">Accessibility page</a>.</p> =20 <h3>Tables</h3> <ul> <li>Ensure that the table is in an editable Word, RTF, or LaTeX format.= </li> <li>Include a brief title (above) and footnotes (below) the table.</li> <li>Avoid multipart tables (Table 1A, Table 1B).</li> </ul> <table class=3D"table"> <thead> <tr style=3D"background-color: #f5f5f5; border-bottom: 1px solid #5= B5B5B;"> <th> </th> <th>Raster</th> <th>Vector</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td>Definition</td> <td>Images composed of pixels (also called =E2=80=9Cbitmapped= =E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9Cpixelmapped=E2=80=9D images). Use for non=E2=80=93lin= e art images, e.g., scanned artwork, digital photographs, complex imagery, = and color effects such as blended colors, shading, shadows, and gradients.<= /td> <td>Images composed of paths (points, lines, and curves) that a= re created with mathematical formulas. Use for line art images, e.g., graph= s, polygons, logos, illustrations, circles, and ellipses, all predominantly= composed of solid colors with sharp lines and contrasts.</td> </tr> <tr> <td>Sample</td> <td><img alt=3D"" src=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/images/= authors/raster-1633446220407.png"> <ul> <li>Notice pixelation and blurriness of numbers,and jag= ged edges of lines. For details, magnify image by using zoom function.</li> </ul> </td> <td><img alt=3D"" src=3D"https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/images/= authors/vector-1633446220423.png"> <ul> <li>Notice continuous smooth paths of numbers and lines= . For details, magnify image by using zoom function.</li> </ul> </td> </tr> <tr> <td>Resolution</td> <td> <ul> <li>Graphics are resolution dependent; scaling to diffe= rent sizes results in quality loss.</li> <li>Images with large dimensions will maintain large fi= le sizes.</li> </ul> </td> <td> <ul> <li>Graphics are <strong>not</strong> resolution depend= ent; they can be scaled to any size without quality loss.</li> <li>Images with large dimensions will maintain smaller = file sizes.</li> </ul> </td> </tr> <tr> <td>File types</td> <td> <ul> <li>JPGs, GIFs, TIFFs, and EPSs/PDFs originating from r= aster programs.</li> <li>Common programs: Adobe Photoshop, Corel Painter, Sk= etchBook Pro, and GIMP.</li> </ul> </td> <td> <ul> <li>AIs, and PPTs/EPSs/PDFs originating from vector pro= grams.</li> <li>Common programs: Adobe Illustrator, Corel DRAW, Ske= tch, PowerPoint, and Canva.</li> </ul> </td> </tr> <tr> <td>Requirements and tips</td> <td> <ul> <li>300 ppi for grayscale or color images with no type = or lettering, 600=E2=80=93900 ppi for grayscale or color images with type, = and 1000=E2=80=931200 ppi for line art, e.g., bar graphs.</li> <li>Color mode should be RGB.</li> <li>Avoid embedding text in graphics.</li> <li>Raster-based files can have low resolution in the p= ublished article, as they cannot be altered in size without losing quality.= </li> </ul> </td> <td> <ul> <li>300 dpi for grayscale or color images with no type = or lettering, 600=E2=80=93900 dpi for grayscale or color images with type, = and 1000=E2=80=931200 dpi for line art, e.g., bar graphs.</li> <li>Color mode should be RGB.</li> <li>Best for creating text in graphics.</li> <li>Vector-based files provide maximum flexibility for = sizing your figures, as they can be altered in size while maintaining high-= quality resolution.</li> </ul> </td> </tr> <tr style=3D"border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;"></tr> </tbody> </table> =20 <h3>Figure legends</h3> <p>Include figure legends immediately after referencing the figure in = the manuscript. Ensure that figure legends adhere to the following guidelin= es: </p> <ul> <li>For figures with multiple panels, the first sentence of the legend shou= ld be a brief overview of the entire figure. Explicitly reference and descr= ibe each panel at least once in the figure legend.</li> <li>Include clearly labeled error bars in all graphs and describe them = ;in the figure legend.</li> <li>State whether a number that follows the =C2=B1 sign is a standard error= (SEM) or a standard deviation (SD).</li> <li>When applicable, provide the <em>P </em>value, magnification, or scale = bar information. </li> <li>Indicate the number of independent data points (<em>N</em>) represented= in a graph in the legend.</li> <li>Ensure that numerical axes on all graphs go to 0, except for log axes.<= /li> </ul> <h3>References</h3> <p>References must be in PNAS style and numbered in the order they appear i= n the text. Unpublished abstracts presented at meetings or references to = =E2=80=9Cdata not shown=E2=80=9D are not permitted. <span>References must n= ot cite retracted articles except when referring to the retraction. PNAS ex= pects authors to cite relevant works and relies on the editors and reviewer= s to note potential omissions during peer review. The journal reserves the = right to ask authors to add relevant citations after publication as determi= ned by editorial review.</span></p> <p>Cite references in numerical order as they appear in text, and include a= ll references cited in the main text in the main manuscript file. Include a= separate citation list for references cited in the SI. Tables and figures = will be inserted in the text where first cited; number references in these = sections accordingly.</p> <ul> <li>Include the full title for each cited article. Use MEDLINE/PubMed abbre= viations of journal titles or use the full journal title for any journals n= ot indexed in MEDLINE.</li> <li>If there are more than five authors, list the first author=E2=80=99s na= me followed by <em>et al</em>.</li> <li>Provide volume numbers for journal articles as applicable; provide DOI = numbers if volume numbers are not available.</li> <li>Provide page ranges for journal articles and book chapters.</li> <li>Provide date of access for online sources.</li> <li>If an article has been accepted for publication but is not yet publishe= d, note in-press status and include a DOI number when possible.</li> </ul> <table class=3D"table"> <tbody> <tr style=3D"background-color: #f5f5f5; border-bottom: 1px solid #5B5B5B;"> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Source</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Example</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Journal articles</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>10. J.-M. Neuhaus, L. Sticher, F. Meins, Jr., T. Boller, A short C-termi= nal sequence is necessary and sufficient for the targeting of chitinases to= the plant vacuole. <em>Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. U.S.A</em>. <strong>88</stro= ng>, 10362=E2=80=9310366 (1991).</p> <p>C. Corsello <em>et al.</em>, <em>FOXP1</em>-related intellectual disabil= ity syndrome: A recognizable entity. <em>J. Med. Genet.</em>, in press.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Research datasets</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>12. E. van Sebille, M. Doblin, Data from =E2=80=9CDrift in ocean current= s impacts intergenerational microbial exposure to temperature.=E2=80=9D Fig= share. Available at <a href=3D"https://dx.doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.31785= 34.v2"> https://dx.doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.3178534.v2.</a> Deposited 15= April 2016.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Articles or chapters in books</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>14. A. V. S. Hill, =E2=80=9CHLA associations with malaria in Africa: Som= e implications for MHC evolution=E2=80=9D in Molecular Evolution of the <em= >Major Histocompatibility Complex</em>, J. Klein, D. Klein, Eds. (Springer,= 1991), pp. 403=E2=80=93420.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Preprints</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>15. H. Luetkens <em>et al.</em>, Electronic phase diagram of the LaO1-xF= xFeAs superconductor. arXiv [Preprint] (2008). <a href=3D"https://arxiv.org= /abs/0806.3533">https://arxiv.org/abs/0806.3533</a> (accessed 6 Novemb= er 2020).</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Conference proceedings</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>7. C. Trepo, =E2=80=9CModelization of the epidemic=E2=80=9D in <em>Proce= edings of the Twelfth International Symposium on Viral Hepatitis and Liver = Disease</em>, H. Alter, J. Maynard, W. Szmuness, Eds. (Franklin Institute P= ress, Philadelphia, PA, 2006), pp. 809=E2=80=93810.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Archived code</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>2. C. Reynaud <em>et al</em>., Tomography. Zenodo. <a href=3D"https://do= i.org/10.5281/zenodo.3712368">https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3712368</a>. D= eposited 15 July 2020.</p> </td> </tr> <tr style=3D"border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;"></tr> <tr> <td valign=3D"top"> <p><strong>Theses or dissertations</strong></p> </td> <td valign=3D"top"> <p>3. D. P. Hua, "Unique allosteric mechanism regulating pr= otein-protein interaction through phosphorylation: A case study of the conf= ormational changes in the syk tandem sh2 protein," PhD thesis, Purdue Unive= rsity, West Lafayette, IN (2016); Available from <a href=3D"https://docs.li= b.purdue.edu/open_access_dissertations/1367/">https://docs.lib.purdue.edu/o= pen_access_dissertations/1367</a></p> </td> </tr> <tr style=3D"border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;"></tr> </tbody> </table> =20 <a id=3D"supporting-information" name=3D"supporting-information"></= a> <h3>Supporting information</h3> <p><strong>SI will be published as provided by the authors and will not be = edited or typeset.</strong></p> <p>SI file formats:</p> <ul> <li><em>SI Appendix</em>: Supply a single PDF, combining all text, fig= ures, tables, movie legends, and SI references. <a href=3D"https://www.pnas= .org/pb-assets/authors/PNAStemplateSI-1755285180080.docx">See the PNAS SI t= emplate</a>.</li> <li><em>Datasets</em>: Supply XLSX, RTF, PDF, CSV, GZ, or TXT files.</li> <li><em>Movies</em>: Supply AVI, MOV, WMV, GIF, or MPEG files at the desire= d reproduction size and length. Movies are limited to 10 MB in size. Includ= e a brief legend for each movie in the SI Appendix.</li> <li><em>Software</em>: Supply source code or scripts in native file types.<= /li> </ul> <p>When preparing SI files, please note the following:</p> <ul> <li>The main text of the paper must stand on its own without the SI.</li> <li>SI is referred to in the text and cannot be altered by authors after ac= ceptance.</li> <li>Refer to the SI Appendix in the manuscript at an appropriate point in t= he text. Number supporting figures and tables starting with S1, S2, etc. Re= ferences should be cited in numerical order as they appear in the SI; do no= t cite main-text references in the SI and vice versa.</li> </ul> <p>If you choose to place detailed materials and methods in an SI Appendix,= you must provide sufficient detail in the main-text methods to enable a re= ader to follow the logic of the procedures and results. The main article te= xt also must reference the SI methods.</p> <p>If a paper is fundamentally a study of a new method or technique, then t= he methods must be described completely in the main text.</p> <a id=3D"journal-cover-images" name=3D"journal-cover-images"></a> <h2>Journal Cover Images</h2> <p>Authors are invited to submit scientifically interesting and visually ar= resting images to be considered for our cover (see our <a href=3D"http= s://www.pnas.org/content/by/year">archive</a>). Illustrations need not appe= ar in the article but should be representative of the work. Images should b= e original, and authors must grant PNAS a License to Publish. AI-generated = images and stock photography will not be considered.</p> <p>To submit an image for consideration for the PNAS cover:</p> <ul> <li>Submit the image with your manuscript through the PNAS manuscript submi= ssions system, or email the image to <a href=3D"mailto:PNASCovers@pnas.nas.= edu">PNASCovers@pnas.nas.edu</a>. Contact PNAS for instructions on submitti= ng large files.</li> <li>Include a brief lay-language caption (50=E2=80=9360 words) and credit i= nformation (e.g., Image credit:...).</li> <li>Submit an image that is 21.59 cm wide by 27.94 cm high.</li> <li>Submit the file in EPS or TIFF format and use RGB color mode.</li> </ul> <p>The deadline for cover submissions is when your proof corrections are re= turned. If you choose to submit an image outside the online submission syst= em, make sure you include the manuscript number, author name, phone, a= nd email in your submission. Authors who submit will be notified either way= if the submission has been selected for a cover.</p> <!--end pagefulltext--> </div> </div> =20 </section> =20 <div><div class=3D"pb-dropzone" data-pb-dropzone=3D"col-0" title=3D= "col-0"></div></div> </main> =20 =20 =20 =20 <section data-widget-def=3D"ux3-layout-widget" data-widget-id=3D"a2= 0ca6a1-603b-499e-81db-ee1dc31f5224" Content-Type: text/css Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable Content-Location: https://www.pnas.org/products/pnas/releasedAssets/css/build-e922fa55e7480621a7f9.css @charset "utf-8"; .ms-ctn { position: relative; padding: 5px 12px; } .ms-inv { border: 1px solid rgb(204, 0, 0); } .ms-ctn-readonly { cursor: pointer; } .ms-ctn-disabled { cursor: not-allowed; background-color: rgb(238, 238, 238= ); } .ms-ctn-bootstrap-focus, .ms-ctn-bootstrap-focus .ms-res-ctn { border-botto= m-left-radius: 0px; border-bottom-right-radius: 0px; border-color: rgba(82,= 168, 236, 0.8) !important; box-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.075) 0px 1px 1px in= set, rgba(82, 168, 236, 0.6) 0px 0px 8px !important; } .ms-ctn-focus { border-color: rgb(102, 175, 233); outline: 0px; box-shadow:= rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.075) 0px 1px 1px inset, rgba(102, 175, 233, 0.6) 0px 0px 8= px; } .ms-ctn input { border: 0px; box-shadow: none; transition: none; outline: 0= px; display: block; padding: 0px; line-height: 1.42857; margin: 1px 0px; wi= dth: 100%; } .ms-ctn .ms-sel-ctn input { float: left; } .ms-ctn-disabled input { cursor: not-allowed; background-color: rgb(238, 23= 8, 238); } .ms-ctn .ms-input-readonly { cursor: pointer; } .ms-ctn .ms-empty-text { color: rgb(221, 221, 221); } .ms-ctn input:focus { border: 0px; box-shadow: none; transition: none; back= ground: rgb(255, 255, 255); } .ms-ctn .ms-trigger { top: 0px; width: 25px; height: 100%; position: absolu= te; right: 0px; background: transparent; border-left: 1px solid rgb(204, 20= 4, 204); cursor: pointer; } .ms-ctn .ms-trigger .ms-trigger-ico { display: inline-block; width: 0px; he= ight: 0px; vertical-align: top; border-top: 4px solid rgb(51, 51, 51); bord= er-right: 4px solid transparent; border-left: 4px solid transparent; conten= t: ""; margin-left: 8px; margin-top: 15px; } .ms-ctn .ms-trigger:hover { background-color: rgb(230, 230, 230); } .ms-ctn .ms-trigger:hover .ms-trigger-ico { background-position: 0px -4px; = } .ms-ctn-disabled .ms-trigger { cursor: not-allowed; background-color: rgb(2= 38, 238, 238); } .ms-ctn-bootstrap-focus { border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(204, 204, 204); } .ms-res-ctn { width: 100%; display: block; overflow-y: auto; } .ms-res-ctn .ms-res-group { line-height: 23px; text-align: left; padding: 2= px 5px; font-weight: 700; border-bottom: 1px dotted rgb(204, 204, 204); bor= der-top: 1px solid rgb(204, 204, 204); background: rgb(243, 237, 255); colo= r: rgb(51, 51, 51); } .ms-res-ctn .ms-res-item { line-height: 25px; text-align: left; padding: 2p= x 5px; color: rgb(102, 102, 102); cursor: pointer; } .ms-res-ctn .ms-res-item-grouped { padding-left: 15px; } .ms-res-ctn .ms-res-odd { background: rgb(250, 250, 250); } .ms-res-ctn .ms-res-item-active { background-color: rgb(245, 245, 245); } .ms-res-ctn .ms-res-item-disabled { color: rgb(204, 204, 204); cursor: defa= ult; } .ms-sel-ctn { overflow: auto; line-height: 18px; padding-right: 25px; } .ms-no-trigger .ms-sel-ctn { padding-right: 0px; } .ms-sel-ctn .ms-sel-item { background: rgb(243, 243, 243); color: rgb(153, = 153, 153); float: left; font-size: 12px; padding: 3px 5px; border-radius: 3= px; border: 1px solid rgb(221, 221, 221); margin: 3px 0px 1px; } .ms-sel-ctn .ms-sel-invalid { border-color: rgb(248, 165, 165) !important; = background: rgb(253, 242, 242) !important; } .ms-sel-ctn .ms-sel-item:hover { border: 1px solid rgb(187, 187, 187); } .ms-ctn .ms-sel-item { background: rgb(243, 243, 243); color: rgb(153, 153,= 153); float: left; font-size: 12px; padding: 0px 5px; border-radius: 3px; = border: 1px solid rgb(221, 221, 221); margin: 1px 5px 1px 0px; } .ms-ctn .ms-sel-item:hover { border: 1px solid transparent; } .ms-ctn-focus .ms-sel-item:hover { border: 1px solid rgb(187, 187, 187); } .ms-sel-ctn .ms-sel-text { background: rgb(255, 255, 255); color: rgb(102, = 102, 102); padding-right: 0px; margin-left: 0px; font-size: 14px; font-weig= ht: 400; } .ms-as-string .ms-sel-text { border-color: transparent; } .ms-res-ctn .ms-res-item em { font-style: normal; background: rgb(86, 86, 8= 6); color: rgb(255, 255, 255); } .ms-sel-ctn .ms-sel-text:hover { background: rgb(255, 255, 255); } .ms-sel-ctn .ms-sel-item-active { border: 1px solid red; background: rgb(11= 7, 117, 117); } .ms-stacked .ms-sel-item { float: inherit; } .ms-sel-ctn .ms-sel-item .ms-close-btn { width: 7px; #007aff; } .swiper-container { margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; position: relati= ve; overflow: hidden; list-style: none; padding: 0px; z-index: 1; } .swiper-container-vertical > .swiper-wrapper { flex-direction: column; } .swiper-wrapper { position: relative; width: 100%; height: 100%; z-index: 1= ; display: flex; transition-property: transform; box-sizing: content-box; } .swiper-container-android .swiper-slide, .swiper-wrapper { transform: trans= lateZ(0px); } .swiper-container-multirow > .swiper-wrapper { flex-wrap: wrap; } .swiper-container-multirow-column > .swiper-wrapper { flex-flow: column wra= p; } .swiper-container-free-mode > .swiper-wrapper { transition-timing-function:= ease-out; margin: 0px auto; } .swiper-container-pointer-events { touch-action: pan-y; } .swiper-container-pointer-events.swiper-container-vertical { touch-action: = pan-x; } .swiper-slide { flex-shrink: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; position: relati= ve; transition-property: transform; } .swiper-slide-invisible-blank { visibility: hidden; } .swiper-container-autoheight, .swiper-container-autoheight .swiper-slide { = height: auto; } .swiper-container-autoheight .swiper-wrapper { align-items: flex-start; tra= nsition-property: transform, height; } .swiper-container-3d { perspective: 1200px; } .swiper-container-3d .swiper-cube-shadow, .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slid= e, .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-bottom, .swiper-container-3d .= swiper-slide-shadow-left, .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-right, = .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-top, .swiper-container-3d .swiper= -wrapper { transform-style: preserve-3d; } .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-bottom, .swiper-container-3d .swi= per-slide-shadow-left, .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-right, .sw= iper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-top { position: absolute; left: 0px;= top: 0px; width: 100%; height: 100%; pointer-events: none; z-index: 10; } .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-left { background-image: linear-g= radient(270deg, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), transparent); } .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-right { background-image: linear-= gradient(90deg, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), transparent); } .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-top { background-image: linear-gr= adient(0deg, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), transparent); } .swiper-container-3d .swiper-slide-shadow-bottom { background-image: linear= -gradient(rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), transparent); } 575.98px) { .landing-page__hero__footer__scroll { left: 0px; right: 0px; bottom: 11.3= 125rem; } } @media (min-width: 576px) { .landing-page__hero__footer__scroll { bottom: 4.375rem; } } .landing-page__hero__footer__scroll__icon { display: none; color: rgb(255, = 255, 255); font-size: 3.75rem; cursor: pointer; } @media (max-width: 575.98px) { .landing-page__hero__footer__scroll__icon { font-size: 2.8125rem; } } .landing-page__hero__footer__scroll__icon:hover, .landing-page__hero__foote= r__scroll__icon:visited { color: rgb(255, 255, 255); text-decoration: none;= } .landing-page__hero .swiper-control-btn { width: 1.25rem; height: 1.25rem; = font-size: 0.9rem; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: cen= ter; margin-top: 0.6rem !important; } .landing-page__hero .swiper-control-btn .icon-play { display: none; } .landing-page__hero .swiper-control-btn.paused .icon-play { display: inline= ; } .landing-page__hero .swiper-control-btn.paused .icon-pause { display: none;= } .landing-page__hero .swiper-pagination { display: flex; justify-content: ce= nter; align-items: center; flex-direction: column; left: 3rem; width: auto;= height: 100%; } @media (max-width: 767.98px) { .landing-page__hero .swiper-pagination { left: 1rem; } } @media (max-width: 575.98px) { .landing-page__hero .swiper-pagination { bottom: 2.5rem; } } .landing-page__hero .swiper-pagination-bullet { background-color: rgb(255, = 255, 255); transition: 0.3s ease-out; opacity: 1; margin: 0.25rem !importan= t; } .landing-page__hero .swiper-pagination-bullet-active { border: 0.25rem soli= d rgb(255, 255, 255); background-color: transparent; height: 1.125rem; widt= h: 1.125rem; } .landing-page .font-matters__aside { } .main-title--square-back-decorat= ed { font-size: 1.75rem; } .landing-page .font-matters__qaa .card { margin: 0px; flex-direction: row-r= everse; } .landing-page .font-matters__qaa .card__image { height: 6rem; width: 6rem; = } @media (max-width: 1239.98px) { .landing-page .font-matters__qaa .card__image { height: 5rem; width: 5rem= ; } } .landing-page .font-matters__qaa .card__title { font-size: 1rem; } .landing-page .font-matters__qaa .card-content { flex-grow: 1; } .toc__section .card { padding-bottom: 1rem; margin-bottom: 2rem; } .toc__section .card-body, .toc__section .card-content { max-width: 100%; } .to-section { font-size: 2.125rem; } .to-section::before { background-color: rgba(28, 117, 188, 0.18); } .to-section--sub { font-size: 1.75rem; } .to-section--sub::before { background-color: rgba(204, 204, 204, 0.2); } .toc .sections-navigation { max-width: 15.625rem; } i.section-info { font-size: 0.8em; } .sections-navigation__container .extraData { border-top-right-radius: 0.125= rem; border-top-left-radius: 0.125rem; } .sections-navigation__nav { border-bottom-right-radius: 0.125rem; border-bo= ttom-left-radius: 0.125rem; } .special-feature { background-color: rgba(28, 117, 188, 0.05); font-family:= Montserrat, serif; margin-bottom: 3.1875rem; } .special-feature__header { background: rgb(28, 117, 187); color: rgb(255, 2= 55, 255); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase;= padding: 0.625rem 0.9375rem 0.625rem 1.25rem; } .special-feature__header.to-section::before { background: unset; } .special-feature__body { padding: 0px 1.0625rem 1.375rem 1.125rem; } .special-feature__body .card { border-bottom: 0.0625rem solid rgb(204, 204,= 204); padding-bottom: 1.5625rem; } .special-feature__body .card:not(:last-child) { margin-bottom: 0px; } .special-feature__title { color: rgb(0, 0, 0); font-size: 2.25rem; font-wei= ght: 600; padding: 1.75rem 0.9375rem 0.4375rem 1.25rem; } .special-feature .card-contribs, .special-feature .card__abstract, .special= -feature .card__meta { font-family: "Open Sans", sans-serif; } .advanced-search__tips { background-color: rgb(246, 246, 246); } .author-center__title { color: rgb(39, 39, 39); font-size: 1.75rem; font-we= ight: 600; font-family: Montserrat, serif; } .author-center-menu-trigger { margin-top: -3rem; } .author-center__content { color: rgb(102, 102, 102); font-size: 1.125rem; f= ont-weight: 400; margin-top: 1.5rem; line-height: 2.25rem; } .author-center__content a { text-decoration: underline; } .author-center__side-menu { background-color: rgb(246, 246, 246); padding: = 0.625rem 1rem; } .author-center__side-menu .collapse { overflow-y: hidden; } .author-center__side-menu__title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 700; font-= family: Montserrat, serif; color: rgb(11, 11, 11); line-height: 1.5rem; mar= gin: 1.25rem 0px 0px; display: block; position: relative; } .author-center__side-menu__title:hover, .author-center__side-menu__title[ar= ia-expanded=3D"true"] { color: rgb(31, 117, 185); text-decoration: none; } .author-center__side-menu__title:hover::before, .author-center__side-menu__= title[aria-expanded=3D"true"]::before { background-color: rgb(31, 117, 185)= ; } .author-center__side-menu__title::before { content: ""; left: -1rem; top: 0= .3rem; height: 1rem; width: 0.25rem; background-color: rgb(189, 189, 189); = border-radius: 0.0625rem; display: block; position: absolute; } .author-center__side-menu__sub-title { font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700= ; color: rgb(102, 102, 102); font-family: "Open Sans", sans-serif; line-hei= ght: 1.25rem; text-transform: uppercase; margin: 0.8125rem 0px; display: bl= ock; } .author-center__sub-title { color: rgb(11, 11, 11); font-weight: 700; font-= family: Montserrat, serif; font-size: 1rem; } .author-center__section { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-conte= nt: space-between; } @media (max-width: 575.98px) { .author-center__section { flex-direction: column-reverse; align-items: fl= ex-start; } .author-center__section img { height: 5rem; width: 5rem; serif; } .commerce-header { font-weight: 700; } .confirmation__table th { font-family: Montserrat, serif; } .checkoutProcessLeftCol:not(.no-buying) { background-color: transparent; } .loading-overlay.is-loading { display: block; } [data-notification=3D"acceptTermsConditions"] .custom-control-label { color= : rgb(39, 39, 39); display: block; } .modal .change-password-form { 0.3rem 2rem; } .static-table tbody tr { border-bottom: 0.0625rem solid rgb(189, 189, 189);= } .authorcenter blockquote, .static-page blockquote { margin-left: 2.5rem; pa= dding-left: 1.5rem; margin-top: 1rem; border-left: 0.0625rem solid rgb(204,= 204, 204); opacity: 0.9; } .brochure__btn a { font-size: 0.875rem; } .about-page p { color: rgb(0, 0, 0); font-size: 1.125rem; } .about-card { margin-bottom: 1rem; margin-right: 1rem; box-shadow: rgb(189,= 189, 189) 0.1875rem 0.1875rem 0px 0px; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border: 0.0625= rem solid rgb(189, 189, 189); width: 30%; } @media (max-width: 767.98px) { .about-card { width: 44%; margin-left: 1rem; } } @media (max-width: 575.98px) { .about-card { width: 100%; } } .about-card:hover { background: rgb(31, 117, 185); color: https://www.pnas.org/pb-assets/styles/pnas-styles-1766506067190.css @charset "utf-8"; .authorcenter h1 { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1.875rem; font-weigh= t: bold; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); text-decoration: none; margin-top: 0px; margi= n-bottom: 10px; } .authorcenter h2, h2 a { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1.5rem; font-w= eight: 600; color: rgb(212, 55, 40); text-decoration: none; line-height: 1.= 6; margin-bottom: 10px; } .authorcenter h3 { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight= : 600; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); text-decoration: none; margin-bottom: 10px; } .authorcenter h4 { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: b= old; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); margin-bottom: 1rem; } .authorcenter paragraph { font-size: 1.5rem; line-height: 1.6; font-weight:= 400; font-family: "Open Sans"; color: rgb(38, 38, 38); } @media screen and (max-width: 768px) { .authorcenter h1 { font-size: 1.625rem; } .authorcenter h2 { font-size: 1.375rem; line-height: 1.6; } } .rss h2 { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; } .rss h4 { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700; } .subscriptions h3 { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: = 600; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); text-decoration: none; margin-bottom: 10px; } .footnote { font-size: 0.75rem; } .subs h2 { color: rgb(0, 0, 0); } .menu-toggle-button { background: rgb(28, 117, 188); color: rgb(255, 255, 2= 55); text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.813rem; line-height: 16px; padding= : 8px 12px; margin-bottom: 15px; display: block; text-align: center; } .check-btn input { margin-right: 100px; } .check-btn label { display: inline-block; } .check-btn input { display: none; } .check-btn input:checked ~ .menu-toggle { display: block; } @media (min-width: 992px) { .check-btn label { display: none; } } @media (max-width: 991px) { .menu-toggle { display: none; } } .slideshow .card-slide__desc { font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.25rem; } .slideshow a { text-decoration: none; } .menu-ac { margin: 0px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 20px; list-style: none; } .menu-ac > li { margin-top: 5px; } .menu-ac > li > a { color: rgb(0, 90, 150); text-decoration: none; font-siz= e: 0.938rem; font-weight: 500; font-family: Montserrat; } .menu-ac > li > a:hover { text-decoration: underline; } .navlist-ac { position: sticky; top: 3rem; } .card-ac { position: relative; display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-w= idth: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);= background-clip: border-box; border: 1px solid rgb(221, 221, 221); margin-= bottom: -1px; } .card-header-ac { padding: 0.75rem 1.25rem; margin: 0px; background-color: = rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03); border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(221, 221, 221); font-size= : 1.125rem; } .card-body-ac { flex: 1 1 auto; min-height: 1px; padding: 1.25rem; } .card-title-ac { margin: 0px 0px 0.75rem; color: rgb(212, 55, 40); } .card-about { text-align: center; border: 2px solid rgb(224, 224, 224); hei= ght: 200px; box-shadow: rgb(224, 224, 224) 5px 10px 8px; margin-bottom: 20p= x; overflow: hidden; } .card-about:hover, .card-about:focus { background: rgb(28, 117, 188); borde= r-color: rgb(28, 117, 188); } .card-about-link:hover { text-decoration: none; } .card-about-title { font-weight: 900; margin-top: 40px; padding-bottom: 20p= x; } .card-about-text { margin-left: 0px; } .card-about:hover .card-about-title, .card-about:hover .card-about-text { c= olor: rgb(255, 255, 255); } .navbar-light .navbar-nav .nav-link { color: rgb(11, 11, 11); } .navbar-light .navbar-nav .nav-link:focus, .navbar-light .navbar-nav .nav-l= ink:hover { color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.9); } .back-to-top__action__ring-bg { stroke: rgb(221, 221, 221); } section.simple-banner { background: linear-gradient(0deg, rgba(255, 255, 25= 5, 0), rgba(255, 255, 255, 0)), url("/pb-assets/images/authors/author-cente= r-banner-bg.png") center center no-repeat !important; } ul.do-pub-list { list-style: none; padding-left: 0px; } .media-menu { padding-bottom: 30px; } .media-menu .author-center__side-menu { background: rgb(255, 255, 255); pad= ding: 0px; } .media-menu .author-center__side-menu li { margin-bottom: 10px; } .media-menu .author-center__side-menu__title::before { display: none; } .media-menu .author-center__side-menu__title { color: rgb(203, 85, 53); mar= gin: 0px; border: 1px solid rgb(224, 224, 224); padding: 15px 20px; } .media-menu .author-center__side-menu .collapse.show, .media-menu .author-c= enter__side-menu .collapsing { border: 1px solid rgb(224, 224, 224); margin= -top: -1px; padding: 10px 20px; } .card-pitn { width: 100%; height: 100%; position: relative; border: 1px sol= id rgb(0, 0, 0); box-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2) 0px 4px 8px 0px; } .pitn-description { padding: 20px; } .pitn-description h4 { padding-bottom: 10px; } .bottom-align-text { position: absolute; bottom: 10px; right: 10px; } #video-container { position: relative; padding-bottom: 56.25%; height: 0px;= } .responsive-iframe { max-width: 560px; max-height: 315px; position: absolut= e; inset: 0px; width: 100%; height: 100%; border-width: medium; border-styl= e: none; border-color: currentcolor; border-image: initial; } .video-holder { display: inline-block; width: 100%; height: 100%; max-width= : 560px; max-height: 315px; } .ebm-list li { line-height: 2; } .rss-list li { line-height: 2; list-style: none; } .btn-center-subs { display: flex; place-content: center; } .well-subs { min-height: 20px; padding: 0px 15px; margin-bottom: 20px; back= ground-color: rgb(245, 245, 245); border: 1px solid rgb(227, 227, 227); bor= der-radius: 4px; width: 100%; } .subs-menu .author-center__side-menu { padding: 0px; } .subs-menu .author-center__side-menu li { margin-bottom: 10px; } .subs-menu .author-center__side-menu__title::before { display: none; } .subs-body h4 { text-align: center; margin-top: 20px; } .subs-body i { color: rgb(0, 0, 0); } .card-subs { text-align: center; margin-bottom: 20px; height: 100%; overflo= w: hidden; padding: 20px; } .card-subs-link:hover { text-decoration: none; } .card-subs:hover h4, .card-subs:hover i { color: rgb(28, 117, 188); } .card-librarian { position: relative; display: flex; } .card-librarian_content { margin: 0px; padding-top: 20px; } .card-librarian_title { padding-bottom: 20px; } .message_404 h1 { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1.875rem; } .message_404 p { font-family: "Open Sans"; font-size: 1.125rem; } .periodic_square { position: relative; width: 75%; border: 1.5px solid; tex= t-align: center; display: flex; } .periodic_square::after { content: ""; display: block; padding-bottom: 100%= ; } .element { position: absolute; width: 100%; padding: 10px; } .atomic_num { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; t= ext-align: left; } .atomic_name { font-family: Montserrat; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 60= 0;
